actualEndpointShapes.GET_metadata_clusters: Bare array of ClusterResponse with cluster_id, field, values, proposed_canonical, priority_score, total_records_affected
actualEndpointShapes.GET_metadata_corrections_history: Paginated with field filter only. Returns entries with timestamp, field, raw_value, canonical_value, evidence, source, action
actualEndpointShapes.GET_metadata_coverage: Returns FieldCoverageResponse per field with confidence_distribution (10 bands), method_distribution, flagged_items
actualEndpointShapes.GET_metadata_issues: Paginated, params: field (required), max_confidence, limit, offset. Returns items with mms_id, raw_value, norm_value, confidence, method
actualEndpointShapes.GET_metadata_methods: Bare array of MethodDistribution (method, count, percentage)
actualEndpointShapes.GET_metadata_publishers: Returns authorities with variant_count, imprint_count, variants[], enrichment IDs, type filter. Read-only
actualEndpointShapes.GET_metadata_unmapped: Bare array of UnmappedValue (raw_value, frequency, confidence, method)
actualEndpointShapes.POST_chat: Works with full two-phase flow. Returns ChatResponseAPI wrapper with ChatResponse containing phase, confidence, candidate_set, suggested_followups, clarification_needed, metadata (polymorphic dict)
actualEndpointShapes.POST_metadata_agent_chat: Accepts field, message, session_id. Returns response, proposals[], clusters[], field, action (analysis|proposals|answer)
actualEndpointShapes.POST_metadata_corrections: Accepts field (place|publisher|agent -- NOT date), raw_value, canonical_value, evidence, source. Returns success, alias_map_updated, records_affected
actualEndpointShapes.POST_metadata_corrections_batch: Accepts corrections array. Returns total_applied, total_skipped, total_records_affected, results
actualEndpointShapes.POST_metadata_primo_urls: Batch URL generation with optional base_url override
actualEndpointShapes.WS_ws_chat: OLD SINGLE-PHASE path. No intent agent, no facets, no phase/confidence. Uses compile_query() directly. Message types: session_created, progress, batch, complete, error

gaps: Zero /diagnostics/* endpoints exist (blocks Query Debugger and DB Explorer)
execution_time_ms computed but NOT in ChatResponse
FacetCounts computed then DISCARDED -- not serialized to ChatResponse
QueryWarning[] not forwarded to frontend
Filter.confidence always null (LLM does not set it)
No CRUD endpoints for publisher authorities (read-only only)
No revert endpoint for corrections
No search/source/date-range params on corrections history
CorrectionRequest.field does not accept 'date'
QueryPlan NOT in ChatResponse (stored in session messages)
Primo URLs not on Candidate objects
surprises: WebSocket /ws/chat uses completely different (old) code path from HTTP /chat
Metadata API coverage endpoint returns 5 fields including agent_name and agent_role (more than 4 assumed)
ChatResponse.metadata is polymorphic untyped dict (different shapes per phase and intent type)
Phase 2 supports 7 intent types: AGGREGATION, METADATA_QUESTION, REFINEMENT, COMPARISON, ENRICHMENT_REQUEST, RECOMMENDATION (stub), NEW_QUERY
AGGREGATION responses include visualization_hint (bar_chart, pie_chart, table)

designPrinciples: Bot-Centered Architecture: chat is the primary surface, everything else supports it
Progressive Disclosure: scholars see clean chat; operators see confidence; developers see query plans
Evidence-First Display: confidence scores and MARC citations are first-class UI elements
Observable by Default: query interpretation, phase transitions, timing visible in real-time
Single Source of Truth: one Primo URL scheme, one query path, one correction workflow
Composable Over Monolithic: reusable components (CandidateCard, ConfidenceBadge, PrimoLink)
architecture.fileStructure: frontend/src/pages/ with Chat.tsx, operator/, diagnostics/, admin/ subdirectories

navigationStructure.sidebarBehavior: Collapses to icons on narrow viewports. Chat page hides sidebar by default (hamburger accessible). Other pages show sidebar.

techStack.charts: Recharts v3 (existing, add Nivo/Observable Plot when needed)
techStack.testing: Vitest + React Testing Library + Playwright + MSW (new)

backendWork.task: Add primo_url to Candidate model OR document batch approach
Upgrade WebSocket handler to two-phase flow
Add search, source, date range to corrections history API
Fix subject evidence value null (populate from DB)
Add subject retry confidence threshold or warning

contradictions.empirical: Report 10-11: WebSocket uses old single-phase compile_query() path. No intent agent, no Phase 2, no facets, no phase/confidence fields. Completely separate code path from HTTP /chat
contradictions.original: Reports 06-07 assume WebSocket supports two-phase flow with phase transitions, confidence, and facets -- used for Chat streaming from Phase 1
contradictions.resolution: Launch Chat with HTTP-only (POST /chat). Defer WebSocket streaming to Phase 4 after handler upgrade (B16: 2-3 days). Phase 1 exit criteria changed to remove WebSocket requirement.

contradictions.empirical: Report 09-11: Filter.confidence is always null in all tested queries. LLM is not asked to produce per-filter confidence. LOW_CONFIDENCE warning can never fire.
contradictions.original: Reports 06-07 specify per-filter confidence visualization in Chat query insight panel and Query Debugger filter contribution view
contradictions.resolution: Drop per-filter confidence from all screens. Show only overall ChatResponse.confidence. Cannot be fixed without changing LLM prompt or computing heuristically.

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: Place and publisher confidence is BINARY -- records are either <0.5 or >=0.95, with the 0.5-0.95 range completely empty. Four-band visualization is misleading.
contradictions.original: Reports 06 assumes four meaningful confidence bands (high/medium/low/very_low) with records distributed across all bands for coverage visualization
contradictions.resolution: Replace four-band with binary (resolved/unresolved) for place and publisher. Keep graduated bands only for dates (has 0.8-0.95 segment with 1,306 records).

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: Only 121 total low-confidence records (69 dates + 19 places + 33 publishers). Workbench will be nearly empty.
contradictions.original: Reports 06-07 assume a substantial volume of issues requiring triage in the Issues Workbench
contradictions.resolution: Reframe from 'issues' to 'improvement opportunities'. Real gaps: 553 Hebrew-script publishers, 4,366 un-aliased agents, 202 unresearched publisher authorities, 44.1% 'other' agent roles. Add method-based filtering instead of confidence slider.

contradictions.empirical: Reports 10-11: ZERO diagnostic endpoints exist. No /diagnostics/query-runs, /diagnostics/labels, /diagnostics/gold-set, /diagnostics/tables. QA database has no HTTP layer at all.
contradictions.original: Reports 06-07 treat diagnostic endpoints as minor additions or assume some exist. Backend prerequisite estimated at 2-3 days.
contradictions.resolution: Backend prerequisite expanded to 4-5 days minimum. Phase 2 must be sequential after Phase 1, not parallel. Total timeline extended from 7 to 8 weeks.

contradictions.empirical: Report 11: FacetCounts are computed by QueryService but NOT serialized into ChatResponse. Data exists internally but is discarded before reaching the frontend.
contradictions.original: Report 06 assumes Phase 2 aggregation charts can be built from API data
contradictions.resolution: Backend micro-task B2: forward FacetCounts in ChatResponse.metadata. Until done, aggregation charts are unbuildable (text responses still work).

contradictions.empirical: Report 10-11: execution_time_ms is computed by QueryService but NOT exposed in ChatResponse. Logged internally only.
contradictions.original: Report 06 specifies execution timing display (compile time, SQL time, total time) in Chat collapsible panel
contradictions.resolution: Backend micro-task B1: add execution_time_ms to ChatResponse.metadata. Small API change.

contradictions.empirical: Report 10: Primo URLs are NOT on Candidate objects. Require separate API call (GET /metadata/records/{mms_id}/primo or POST /metadata/primo-urls batch).
contradictions.original: Report 06 assumes candidate titles link directly to Primo with inline URLs
contradictions.resolution: Use POST /metadata/primo-urls batch call after results load. Cache client-side. Alternatively, add primo_url to Candidate model (B4).

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: Database has 10 tables (adds notes, publisher_authorities, publisher_variants, authority_enrichment)
contradictions.original: Report 06 lists 6 tables for DB Explorer (records, imprints, titles, subjects, languages, agents)
contradictions.resolution: DB Explorer scope expanded to 10 tables.

contradictions.empirical: Report 12: Phase 2 backend work is substantial (all diagnostic endpoints from scratch) and should not compete with Phase 1 backend micro-tasks for developer attention
contradictions.original: Report 07 states Phases 1 and 2 can overlap because they touch different screens and endpoints
contradictions.resolution: Phases 1 and 2 are sequential, not parallel. Timeline extended by 1 week.

contradictions.empirical: Report 10: CorrectionRequest.field accepts only place|publisher|agent -- NOT date
contradictions.original: Reports imply corrections work for all four fields (date, place, publisher, agent)
contradictions.resolution: Date corrections need either a different mechanism or API extension (B17: 0.5 day in Phase 4).

contradictions.empirical: Report 10: GET /metadata/corrections/history only supports field filter. No search, source, or date range params.
contradictions.original: Report 06 specifies filter bar with field, source, search, date range for correction history
contradictions.resolution: Client-side filtering approximates until API params added (B18: 0.5 day in Phase 4).

contradictions.empirical: Report 10-11: QueryPlan is NOT in ChatResponse. It is stored in session messages, accessible via GET /sessions/{session_id}.
contradictions.original: Reports assume QueryPlan is accessible from ChatResponse for debugger and chat insight
contradictions.resolution: Workaround: fetch session and extract plan from most recent assistant message. Or add plan to diagnostics query-run endpoint response.

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: Only 2 methods exist in production data -- place_alias_map (99.3%) and missing (0.7%). No base_clean method records exist for places.
contradictions.original: CLAUDE.md mentions base_clean (0.80) and alias_map (0.95) as place normalization methods
contradictions.resolution: Confidence slider for places is meaningless (only <0.5 or >=0.95 exist). Replace with method-based filtering.

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: ALL 4,366 agents use base_clean method only. 0% are alias-mapped. 44.1% of agent roles are 'other'. This is the largest normalization gap by far.
contradictions.original: Reports 01-07 focus on date/place/publisher normalization gaps
contradictions.resolution: Lead Coverage Dashboard with agent normalization gaps, not date/place/publisher (which are 97%+ resolved). Add agent normalization tab to Issues Workbench.

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: 227 authorities exist but 202 (89%) are 'unresearched' stubs. authority_enrichment table has 0 rows. Only 18 are classified as printing_house.
contradictions.original: Reports mention 228 publisher authorities as a working system
contradictions.resolution: Publisher Authorities screen should emphasize research workflow (classifying stubs, adding enrichment) rather than simple CRUD listing.

anomalies: 553 publisher_norm values contain only non-Latin characters (Hebrew/Yiddish) -- 20.2% of publishers, technically 0.95 confidence but not normalized for cross-lingual search
44.1% of agent roles are 'other' (1,924 of 4,366) -- massive normalization gap
100% of agents use base_clean only, 0% alias-mapped
'frankfurt' (48) and 'frankfurt am main' (30) exist as separate normalized places -- deduplication target
[sine loco] (41) records with unknown place of publication
215 imprints (7.9%) have unknown publishers
38 records have no imprints (Faitlovitch collection manuscripts)
authority_enrichment table is completely empty (0 rows)
89% of publisher authorities (202/227) are unresearched stubs
Date range spans 1244-2025 (~780 years), peak at 18th century (874 records, 32.3%)

place.note: BINARY: no records in 0.5-0.95 range at all

publisher.note: BINARY: no records in 0.5-0.95 range at all

agent.method: ALL 4,366 agents use base_clean only (no alias map)

place.note: No base_clean method in production, only alias_map or missing

featuresDrop.reason: Replaced by React Chat screen with streaming and phase visualization
Replaced by React Query Debugger + DB Explorer
Overly complex; standard query run history suffices
Experimental, low usage; standard labeling flow covers same ground
Merged into CLI as regression subcommand

featuresDrop.item: Dual Primo URL schemes (TAU hardcoded + NLI hardcoded)
featuresDrop.reason: Consolidated to single configurable scheme
Either upgrade to two-phase or replace; currently uses deprecated code path
Filter.confidence is always null; unbuildable
Confidence is binary; medium and low bands are empty

featuresDrop.item: Trend over time on Coverage Dashboard
featuresDrop.reason: No snapshot mechanism exists; requires infrastructure first
No tracking infrastructure exists

featuresDrop.item: Rich System Health features (log viewer, metrics dashboard, interaction viewer)
featuresDrop.reason: No infrastructure exists; defer to later phase
Expose through unified diagnostic API
Environment variable via Vite config

featuresDrop.item: Emoji in CLI and QA Tool output
featuresDrop.reason: Per project code style
Irrelevant to MARC bibliographic records
Currently used only for pie charts; evaluate replacement when complex charts needed

featuresKeep.destination: Issues Workbench, Query Debugger, DB Explorer, Publishers

featuresKeep.feature: Coverage Dashboard (stat cards, coverage bars, gap cards, method charts)
Agent Chat (proposal tables, approve/reject/edit, coverage sidebar)
Correction Review (audit trail, filtering, pagination, export)
TanStack Query patterns (cache invalidation, optimistic updates)
Tailwind CSS design system (confidence color bands)
Smart date display (single year vs range)
False negative search (database search for missing results)
Gold set export + regression runner display
Read-only DB table browser with column search
Intent interpretation with confidence scoring (0.85 threshold)

evidenceShapeIssues: Subject evidence value is always null (Issue #1, Medium severity) -- only matched_against populated
Agent evidence source shows 'marc:unknown' instead of actual MARC tag (Issue #3, Low severity)
Empty-filter queries produce evidence-less candidates with evidence:[] (Issue #5, Low severity)

falseBroadening.description: Subject retry mechanism remaps inapplicable subjects to loosely related ones. 'quantum physics' returns 67 philosophy books. compile_query_with_subject_hints() uses top-100 DB subjects as hints, LLM picks closest match.
falseBroadening.recommendation: Add confidence threshold to retry mapping, or return zero results with suggestion instead of silently broadening

filterConfidenceAlwaysNull.description: All filter objects in QueryPlan have confidence: null. LLM is not asked to produce per-filter confidence. LOW_CONFIDENCE warning can never fire.

noAmbiguityWarningForSingleWords.description: Single-word queries like 'Venice' are interpreted without ambiguity warning. Could be place, subject, or title.

publisherVariantGap.description: Query 'books published by Elsevier' matched 1 of 16 Elzevir-family records. CONTAINS filter LIKE '%elsevier%' only matches modern spelling, misses historical forms (apud danielem elzevirivm, ex officina elseviriana, typis l. elzevirii, etc.). Publisher authority system maps these to 'house of elzevir' but query doesn't search for 'elzevir'.
publisherVariantGap.impact: Users searching modern publisher names may miss most historical records

queryBehaviorObservations: LLM compilation dominates execution time (2-4 seconds per fresh query)
Cache hits eliminate LLM time entirely (152ms for cached query vs 3000+ms fresh)
SQL execution consistently fast (<600ms even for full-table scans)
Place, date, and language filters are reliable and produce correct results
Warning system correctly identifies empty-filter queries
Facet computation provides useful breakdowns (by_place, by_year, by_language, by_publisher, by_century)

risks.mitigation: Strict feature parity checklist; ADD features are Phase 4+

designAdjustments: Launch HTTP-only, defer WebSocket streaming until handler upgraded to two-phase
Drop per-filter confidence display (always null), show only overall confidence
Batch-resolve Primo URLs via POST /metadata/primo-urls, cache client-side
Handle polymorphic ChatResponse.metadata with type guards

Phase 2 exploration: aggregation text, refinement, comparison, enrichment, new query
Candidate objects have all fields for result cards

Keep graduated bands only for dates (0.8-0.95 has 1,306 records)

Date corrections not currently supported via corrections API

timeline.criticalPath: Phase 0 -> Phase 1 -> Phase 2 (backend) -> Phase 2 (frontend) -> Phase 4 -> Phase 5
earlyWins: End of Week 1: Restructured React app with 9-screen skeleton
End of Week 3: Working Chat screen (Streamlit Chat can be retired)
End of Week 5: Working Query Debugger (QA Tool can be retired)
End of Week 8: All old UIs removed, single unified app

phase0.exitCriteria: All 4 existing Workbench pages render at new URLs. Placeholder pages exist for 5 new screens.
phase0.name: Foundation & Scaffolding

deliverables: Chat.tsx with two-phase flow via HTTP
Follow-up chips, example queries, phase indicator
phase1.exitCriteria: Can send NL query via HTTP, see formatted results with evidence, two-phase flow works, overall confidence displayed, clarification prompts render, sessions persist, Primo links work
phase1.name: Chat Screen (HTTP-only)

deliverables: CLI consolidation: merge app/qa.py into app/cli.py as regression subcommand
phase2.exitCriteria: Can run query, see plan+SQL+results; can label TP/FP/FN/UNK; can search for FN; can export gold set and run regression; DB Explorer shows all 10 tables; CLI regression subcommand works
phase2.name: Query Debugger + DB Explorer
phase2.status: MODIFIED: sequential after Phase 1, backend expanded to 4-5 days

deliverables: Publishers.tsx with authority list, type filtering, expandable variants (CRUD requires B13-B14)
Health.tsx with basic status + nav bar indicator (B15)
phase3.name: Admin Screens
reframings: Publisher screen emphasizes research workflow (classifying 202 stubs)
System Health scoped down to basic status only
phase3.status: MODIFIED: split into 3a (read-only publishers parallel with Phase 1) and 3b (CRUD + Health)

deliverables: Cross-screen links (Chat->Workbench 'flag issue', Workbench->AgentChat, Debugger->Workbench for FN)
Shared component finalization (ConfidenceBadge, CandidateCard, PrimoLink, FieldBadge)
phase4.name: Polish, Integration & Testing
phase4.status: MODIFIED: additional tasks from empirical findings

archives: Wizard code, QA DB schema reference, regression runner reference -> archive/retired_streamlit/
phase5.exitCriteria: Single React app serves 9 screens. No Streamlit dependencies. CLAUDE.md updated. Git tagged ui-migration-complete.
phase5.name: Retirement & Cleanup

timeline.reasonForExtraWeek: Diagnostic endpoint backend work larger than estimated; Phases 1 and 2 must be sequential not parallel

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: Place and publisher confidence is BINARY -- records are either <0.5 or >=0.95, with the 0.5-0.95 range completely empty. Four-band visualization is misleading.
contradictions.original: Reports 06 assumes four meaningful confidence bands (high/medium/low/very_low) with records distributed across all bands for coverage visualization
contradictions.resolution: Replace four-band with binary (resolved/unresolved) for place and publisher. Keep graduated bands only for dates (has 0.8-0.95 segment with 1,306 records).

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: Only 121 total low-confidence records (69 dates + 19 places + 33 publishers). Workbench will be nearly empty.
contradictions.original: Reports 06-07 assume a substantial volume of issues requiring triage in the Issues Workbench
contradictions.resolution: Reframe from 'issues' to 'improvement opportunities'. Real gaps: 553 Hebrew-script publishers, 4,366 un-aliased agents, 202 unresearched publisher authorities, 44.1% 'other' agent roles. Add method-based filtering instead of confidence slider.

contradictions.empirical: Reports 10-11: ZERO diagnostic endpoints exist. No /diagnostics/query-runs, /diagnostics/labels, /diagnostics/gold-set, /diagnostics/tables. QA database has no HTTP layer at all.
contradictions.original: Reports 06-07 treat diagnostic endpoints as minor additions or assume some exist. Backend prerequisite estimated at 2-3 days.
contradictions.resolution: Backend prerequisite expanded to 4-5 days minimum. Phase 2 must be sequential after Phase 1, not parallel. Total timeline extended from 7 to 8 weeks.

contradictions.empirical: Report 11: FacetCounts are computed by QueryService but NOT serialized into ChatResponse. Data exists internally but is discarded before reaching the frontend.
contradictions.original: Report 06 assumes Phase 2 aggregation charts can be built from API data
contradictions.resolution: Backend micro-task B2: forward FacetCounts in ChatResponse.metadata. Until done, aggregation charts are unbuildable (text responses still work).

contradictions.empirical: Report 10-11: execution_time_ms is computed by QueryService but NOT exposed in ChatResponse. Logged internally only.
contradictions.original: Report 06 specifies execution timing display (compile time, SQL time, total time) in Chat collapsible panel
contradictions.resolution: Backend micro-task B1: add execution_time_ms to ChatResponse.metadata. Small API change.

contradictions.empirical: Report 10: Primo URLs are NOT on Candidate objects. Require separate API call (GET /metadata/records/{mms_id}/primo or POST /metadata/primo-urls batch).
contradictions.original: Report 06 assumes candidate titles link directly to Primo with inline URLs
contradictions.resolution: Use POST /metadata/primo-urls batch call after results load. Cache client-side. Alternatively, add primo_url to Candidate model (B4).

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: Database has 10 tables (adds notes, publisher_authorities, publisher_variants, authority_enrichment)
contradictions.original: Report 06 lists 6 tables for DB Explorer (records, imprints, titles, subjects, languages, agents)
contradictions.resolution: DB Explorer scope expanded to 10 tables.

contradictions.empirical: Report 12: Phase 2 backend work is substantial (all diagnostic endpoints from scratch) and should not compete with Phase 1 backend micro-tasks for developer attention
contradictions.original: Report 07 states Phases 1 and 2 can overlap because they touch different screens and endpoints
contradictions.resolution: Phases 1 and 2 are sequential, not parallel. Timeline extended by 1 week.

contradictions.empirical: Report 10: CorrectionRequest.field accepts only place|publisher|agent -- NOT date
contradictions.original: Reports imply corrections work for all four fields (date, place, publisher, agent)
contradictions.resolution: Date corrections need either a different mechanism or API extension (B17: 0.5 day in Phase 4).

contradictions.empirical: Report 10: GET /metadata/corrections/history only supports field filter. No search, source, or date range params.
contradictions.original: Report 06 specifies filter bar with field, source, search, date range for correction history
contradictions.resolution: Client-side filtering approximates until API params added (B18: 0.5 day in Phase 4).

contradictions.empirical: Report 10-11: QueryPlan is NOT in ChatResponse. It is stored in session messages, accessible via GET /sessions/{session_id}.
contradictions.original: Reports assume QueryPlan is accessible from ChatResponse for debugger and chat insight
contradictions.resolution: Workaround: fetch session and extract plan from most recent assistant message. Or add plan to diagnostics query-run endpoint response.

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: Only 2 methods exist in production data -- place_alias_map (99.3%) and missing (0.7%). No base_clean method records exist for places.
contradictions.original: CLAUDE.md mentions base_clean (0.80) and alias_map (0.95) as place normalization methods
contradictions.resolution: Confidence slider for places is meaningless (only <0.5 or >=0.95 exist). Replace with method-based filtering.

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: ALL 4,366 agents use base_clean method only. 0% are alias-mapped. 44.1% of agent roles are 'other'. This is the largest normalization gap by far.
contradictions.original: Reports 01-07 focus on date/place/publisher normalization gaps
contradictions.resolution: Lead Coverage Dashboard with agent normalization gaps, not date/place/publisher (which are 97%+ resolved). Add agent normalization tab to Issues Workbench.

contradictions.empirical: Report 08: 227 authorities exist but 202 (89%) are 'unresearched' stubs. authority_enrichment table has 0 rows. Only 18 are classified as printing_house.
contradictions.original: Reports mention 228 publisher authorities as a working system
contradictions.resolution: Publisher Authorities screen should emphasize research workflow (classifying stubs, adding enrichment) rather than simple CRUD listing.

anomalies: 553 publisher_norm values contain only non-Latin characters (Hebrew/Yiddish) -- 20.2% of publishers, technically 0.95 confidence but not normalized for cross-lingual search
44.1% of agent roles are 'other' (1,924 of 4,366) -- massive normalization gap
100% of agents use base_clean only, 0% alias-mapped
'frankfurt' (48) and 'frankfurt am main' (30) exist as separate normalized places -- deduplication target
[sine loco] (41) records with unknown place of publication
215 imprints (7.9%) have unknown publishers
38 records have no imprints (Faitlovitch collection manuscripts)
authority_enrichment table is completely empty (0 rows)
89% of publisher authorities (202/227) are unresearched stubs
Date range spans 1244-2025 (~780 years), peak at 18th century (874 records, 32.3%)

place.note: BINARY: no records in 0.5-0.95 range at all

publisher.note: BINARY: no records in 0.5-0.95 range at all

agent.method: ALL 4,366 agents use base_clean only (no alias map)

place.note: No base_clean method in production, only alias_map or missing

evidenceShapeIssues: Subject evidence value is always null (Issue #1, Medium severity) -- only matched_against populated
Agent evidence source shows 'marc:unknown' instead of actual MARC tag (Issue #3, Low severity)
Empty-filter queries produce evidence-less candidates with evidence:[] (Issue #5, Low severity)

falseBroadening.description: Subject retry mechanism remaps inapplicable subjects to loosely related ones. 'quantum physics' returns 67 philosophy books. compile_query_with_subject_hints() uses top-100 DB subjects as hints, LLM picks closest match.
falseBroadening.recommendation: Add confidence threshold to retry mapping, or return zero results with suggestion instead of silently broadening

filterConfidenceAlwaysNull.description: All filter objects in QueryPlan have confidence: null. LLM is not asked to produce per-filter confidence. LOW_CONFIDENCE warning can never fire.

noAmbiguityWarningForSingleWords.description: Single-word queries like 'Venice' are interpreted without ambiguity warning. Could be place, subject, or title.

publisherVariantGap.description: Query 'books published by Elsevier' matched 1 of 16 Elzevir-family records. CONTAINS filter LIKE '%elsevier%' only matches modern spelling, misses historical forms (apud danielem elzevirivm, ex officina elseviriana, typis l. elzevirii, etc.). Publisher authority system maps these to 'house of elzevir' but query doesn't search for 'elzevir'.
publisherVariantGap.impact: Users searching modern publisher names may miss most historical records

queryBehaviorObservations: LLM compilation dominates execution time (2-4 seconds per fresh query)
Cache hits eliminate LLM time entirely (152ms for cached query vs 3000+ms fresh)
SQL execution consistently fast (<600ms even for full-table scans)
Place, date, and language filters are reliable and produce correct results
Warning system correctly identifies empty-filter queries
Facet computation provides useful breakdowns (by_place, by_year, by_language, by_publisher, by_century)

timeline.criticalPath: Phase 0 -> Phase 1 -> Phase 2 (backend) -> Phase 2 (frontend) -> Phase 4 -> Phase 5
earlyWins: End of Week 1: Restructured React app with 9-screen skeleton
End of Week 3: Working Chat screen (Streamlit Chat can be retired)
End of Week 5: Working Query Debugger (QA Tool can be retired)
End of Week 8: All old UIs removed, single unified app

phase0.exitCriteria: All 4 existing Workbench pages render at new URLs. Placeholder pages exist for 5 new screens.
